mission.title: cloudevents: Extension names become case insensitive in binary encoding
mission.description: Extension names become case insensitive in binary encoding. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current cloudevents setup
steps.description:
Verify your cloudevents version:
CloudEvents is a specification — check your SDK version (e.g., pip show cloudevents)
This feature requires cloudevents as a dependency.

steps.title: Review cloudevents configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant cloudevents configuration:
Extensions are not defined to have case-insensitive names, but the binary encoding puts the extension name in a header. Since header names are case insensitive, their case may be changed by middleware. This makes extensions fairly fragile.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Extension names become case insensitive in binary encoding
steps.description:
A single header would be ok as long as the extensions are actually modeled as sub-properties of something. E.g. if in JSON they were:
```
"extensions": {
  "foo": "bar"
}
```
If we allowed extensions to be top-level json properties then they're more akin to siblings of spec-approved attributes and
```yaml
CE-X: <key>: <value>
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected.
Confirm the feature described in "Extension names become case insensitive in binary encoding" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
A single header would be ok as long as the extensions are actually modeled as sub-properties of something. E.g. if in JSON they were:
```
"extensions": {
  "foo": "bar"
}
```
If we allowed extensions to be top-level json properties then they're more akin to siblings of spec-approved attributes and I would prefer for them to be individual http headers.

prerequisites.description: CloudEvents is a specification — check your SDK version (e.g., pip show cloudevents)
